info.name: Modal Sandbox API (Express)
info.description:
REST API over Modal Sandboxes (Express + TypeScript).

API-compatible with the NestJS version. Set the `baseUrl`, `repoUrl`, and `gitToken`
collection variables before running.
`POST /sandboxes` and `POST /previews` automatically save the returned id into
the `sandboxId` / `previewSandboxId` variables, so the file/exec/tunnel requests
can be run right after with no manual copying.

request.description: Liveness check.

response.name: 400 Bad Request (validation)

request.description: List all sandboxes currently tracked in the in-memory registry.
Returns exitCode (null while running) and current tunnel URLs.
Terminate the sandbox and drop it from the registry.
Run a command and wait for it to finish (buffered). For short commands; not for long-running servers.

request.description: Remove a file or directory (recursive).

item.name: File Toolkit
item.description: AI-friendly file access: partial reads, targeted edits, tree, search, and file management.

request.description: Read a 1-based inclusive line range. Omit start/end for the whole file (files > 2MB require a range). numbered=1 prefixes line numbers. Returns { path, totalLines, start, end, content }.

response.name: Live: Read File (line range) → 200
response.body:
{
  "path": "/workspace/repo/package.json",
  "totalLines": 62,
  "start": 1,
  "end": 12,
  "content": "1	{
2	  "name": "new-test",
3	  "private": true,
4	  "type": "module",
5	  "imports": {
6	    "#/*": "./src/*"
7	  },
8	  "scripts": {
9	    "dev": "vite dev",
10	    "dev:tagger": "ENABLE_TAGGER=true vite dev",
11	    "build": "vite build",
12	    "build:tagger": "ENABLE_TAGGER=true vite build","
}

request.description: String-match edit (like a code editor). 400 if oldString is missing or non-unique unless replaceAll=true. Returns { changed, totalLines, before, after }. Set dryRun=true to preview without writing.
Replace a 1-based inclusive line range with new content.
Insert before the given 1-based line (1 = prepend, beyond EOF = append). Other ops: "delete" {start,end}, "append" {content}, "prepend" {content}.
File metadata: { path, name, type, size, mtime, permissions }.
{ path, exists }.
Recursive folder tree (ignores node_modules/.git/dist/etc). Returns { path, tree (nested), text (ASCII render) }.

response.name: Live: Tree → 200

request.description: grep file contents. Only `query` is required. Returns { matches: [{ file, line, text }], truncated }.

response.name: Live: Search (grep) → 200

request.description: Find files by name (excludes node_modules/.git). Returns { files, truncated }.
mkdir -p.
Move/rename (creates the destination's parent dir).
Copy a file or directory (recursive).
Public HTTPS tunnel URL per exposed (encrypted) port.

FREE path (no Workers for Platforms): deploy the cloned Worker, then bind it to customDomain. Cloudflare creds (apiToken/accountId/zoneId) come from .env by default — add them to the body to override. `env` is injected BOTH as a build-time .env (Vite/import.meta.env) AND as runtime Worker vars (--var). Run a preview/create first so {{sandboxId}} points at an installed Cloudflare-Workers project. Returns { scriptName, url, customDomain }.

Build the cloned app and deploy it into a Workers for Platforms dispatch namespace via wrangler, inside the sandbox. The Cloudflare token is used only for the deploy step and never persisted. Redeploying with the same scriptName updates in place (same URL). Run a preview/create first so {{sandboxId}} points at an installed sandbox.

response.name: 201 Created
400 Bad Request (missing fields)

item.name: Deployments (domain rename)
item.description: Manage the public custom domain of a deployed Worker (the free custom-domain path). Cloudflare creds come from .env.

request.description: List the custom domains currently bound to the Worker named `poc-test`. → { scriptName, domains: [...] }

Change the public URL of the `poc-test` Worker: validates the new subdomain label (≤45, lowercase start, [a-z0-9-], no leading/trailing/consecutive hyphens, reserved blocklist), rejects 409 if the hostname is used by another project, attaches the new domain, and detaches the old one (old URL then 404s). Note: on the free path Cloudflare re-issues the TLS cert for the new hostname, so HTTPS may take a few minutes to serve. → { scriptName, domain, removed: [...] }

response.name: 400 Bad Request (invalid label)

request.description: Spin up a live preview: create sandbox (git + pnpm) -> clone repo -> install -> start dev server -> return the public tunnel URL. Only `repoUrl` is required; `token` is needed for private repos. Defaults: port 5173, `pnpm install`, `pnpm run dev`.

response.name: 502 Bad Gateway (clone/install failed)
